summary: Gruber makes the case that Apple Watch Edition models will start around $10,000 and range up to $20,000, far higher than most tech observers expect. He argues that bands serve as pricing tiers within each collection, with the steel Apple Watch ranging from $700 to over $1,000 depending on band choice. He believes Apple is deliberately withholding a gold Link Bracelet as a surprise reveal that will command the highest prices. His core argument is that Apple Watch Edition should be priced as a luxury watch, not a tech product, and that Apple is entering the Swiss watch market head-to-head on price while competing on features.
key_points: Bands are not mere accessories but pricing tier signifiers — the nicer bands won't be sold separately at retail, and the order on Apple's website likely reflects ascending price
The steel Apple Watch will span at least five pricing tiers from Sport Band (~$700) to Link Bracelet (well over $1,000), with a possible 'black tax' premium for space gray/black models
Apple Watch Edition pricing should be understood through the lens of the luxury watch industry, where gold watches cost $10-15K more than steel equivalents regardless of raw gold content
Apple is likely withholding a gold Link Bracelet as an April surprise, which would push Edition pricing to $20,000
Apple's strategy is not to disrupt luxury watch pricing but to match it while offering disruptive features
money_quotes: Apple Watch Edition is not a tech product, so don't try to price it like one.
Don't try to guess the price of the Edition models based on the amount of gold they contain.
The nicer bands aren't accessories that Apple hopes you'll tack onto your purchase; they're signifiers of how much you paid for your stainless steel or gold Apple Watch.

They're not entering the market against Rolex, Omega, and the rest of the Swiss luxury watch establishment with disruptive prices. They're entering the market against those companies going head-to-head on pricing, with disruptive (they think) features.
At prices like these, an Apple Watch Edition is not an accessory for your iPhone — your iPhone is an accessory for your Apple Watch Edition.
If you're going to go gold, go gold. Some people buy a gold watch simply because they like it. Others buy a gold watch because they want everyone to know they wear a gold watch.
I should have been asking if $5,000 is too low.
key_insight: Apple Watch Edition should be understood not as a tech product with a gold case but as a luxury watch competing head-to-head with Rolex on price while differentiating on features.
